verses.sanskrit: स रामः रथम् आस्थाय सम्प्रह्Rष्ट सुह्Rज् जनः ।पताकाध्वजसंपन्नं महार्हगुरुधूपितम् ॥२-१७-१॥
verses.text:
Rama the glorious man, who was with his joyful friends, ascended the chariot and saw 
the city which was decorated with flags and banners, which was perfumed all around by burning excellent 
aloe wood and which was endowed with varied types of people.

verses.sanskrit: अपश्यन् नगरम् श्रीमान् नाना जन समाकुलम् ।स ग्Rहैः अभ्र सम्काशैः पाण्डुरैः उपशोभितम् ॥२-१७-२॥
verses.text:
That Rama went through the royal passage which was incensed with aloe wood. That royal 
street was splendid with white houses resembling clouds.

verses.sanskrit: राज मार्गम् ययौ रामः मध्येन अगरु धूपितम् ।चन्दनानाम् च मुख्यानामगुरूणाम् च सम्चयैः ॥२-१७-३॥
verses.text:
Rama entered that excellent royal route, which was filled with sandal, superior aloe-wood, 
excellent perfumes, silk and linen cloth, heaps of them, unpierced pearls, excellent things made of 
crystal, splendid with various kinds of flowers and eatables and which was not congested.

verses.sanskrit: उत्तमानाम् च गन्धानां क्षौमकौशाम्बरस्य च ।अविद्धाभिश्च मुक्ताभिरुत्तमैः स्फाटिकैरपि ॥२-१७-४॥
शोभमानम् असम्बाधम् तम् राज पथम् उत्तमम् ।सम्व्Rतम् विविधैः पण्यैः भक्ष्यैः उच्च अवचैः अपि ॥२-१७-५॥
ददर्श तं राजपथं दिवि देवपथम् यथा ।दध्यक्षतहविर्लाजैर्धूपैरगुरुचम्दनैः ॥२-१७-६॥
verses.text:
Rama saw that royal route like divine passage in heaven. All the road junctions there 
were regularly worshipped with yoghurt, unbroken rice, clarified butter, fried grain, incense, aloe 
and sandal and all types of garlands and perfumes.

verses.sanskrit: नानामाल्योपगंधैश्च सदाभ्यर्चितचत्वरम् ।आशीर् वादान् बहून् श्Rण्वन् सुह्Rद्भिः समुदीरितान् ॥२-१७-७॥
verses.text:
Hearing the various blessing words spoken by his friends, he went acknowledging all those 
people suitably.

verses.sanskrit: यथा अर्हम् च अपि सम्पूज्य सर्वान् एव नरान् ययौ ।पितामहैः आचरितम् तथैव प्रपितामहैः ॥२-१७-८॥
verses.text:
"Get coronated today, take the path followed by your grandfathers and great grandfathers 
and rule the kingdom."

verses.sanskrit: अद्य उपादाय तम् मार्गम् अभिषिक्तः अनुपालय ।यथा स्म लालिताः पित्रा यथा पूर्वैः पितामहैः ॥२-१७-९॥
verses.text:
"If Rama becomes king, we shall be more happy than when his father ruled and also when 
his grandfathers ruled.

verses.sanskrit: ततः सुखतरम् सर्वे रामे वत्स्याम राजनि ।अलमद्य हि भुक्तेन परम अर्थैः अलम् च नः ॥२-१७-१०॥
verses.text:
"If we can see Rama going forth in the city after getting coronated today, it is enough 
of eating or other activities."

verses.sanskrit: यथा पश्याम निर्यान्तम् रामम् राज्ये प्रतिष्ठितम् ।ततः हि न प्रियतरम् न अन्यत् किम्चित् भविष्यति ॥२-१७-११॥
verses.text: "There is no other dearer thing to us than coronation of Rama of unbounded splendour".

verses.sanskrit: यथा अभिषेको रामस्य राज्येन अमित तेजसः ।एताः च अन्याः च सुह्Rदाम् उदासीनः कथाः शुभाः ॥२-१७-१२॥
verses.text:
Rama, though hearing of these good, words spoken by his friends about himself was passive 
and went in royal route.

verses.sanskrit: आत्म सम्पूजनीः श्Rण्वन् ययौ रामः महा पथम् ।न हि तस्मान् मनः कश्चिच् चक्षुषी वा नर उत्तमात् ॥२-१७-१३॥
verses.text:
Though Rama became distant, no one could turn back one's eyes and mind from him, the 
best of men.

verses.sanskrit: नरः शक्नोति अपाक्रष्टुम् अतिक्रान्ते अपि राघवे ।यश्च रामं न पश्येत्तु यं च रामो न पश्यति ॥२-१७-१४॥
verses.text:
All the people blamed the one who did not see Rama and the one who was not seen by Rama. 
Such a person would blame himself of it.

verses.sanskrit: निन्दतः सर्वलोकेषु स्वात्माप्येनम् विगर्हते ।सर्वेषाम् स हि धर्म आत्मा वर्णानाम् कुरुते दयाम् ॥२-१७-१५॥
verses.text:
Rama, the virtuous, had compassion towards all the four classes of men and towards the 
aged people. Hence, they were all devoted to him.

verses.sanskrit: चतुर्णाम् हि वयह्स्थानाम् तेन ते तम् अनुव्रताः ।चत्पुष्पथान् देवपथाम्श्चैत्यान्यायतनानि च ॥२-१७-१६॥
verses.text:
Rama went by crossing road junction, god's paths, sacred trees and temples keeping his 
right side towards them.

verses.sanskrit: प्रदक्षिणम् परिहरन् जगाम नृपतेस्सुतः ।स राज कुलम् आसाद्य महा इन्द्र भवन उपमम् ॥२-१७-१७॥
verses.text:
Rama approached the royal abode and entered his father's palace which was the best palace 
with blazing radiance like the palace of king of celestials on earth. All around the house, there were 
various types turrets looking like Mount Kailasa, as group of clouds, as white heavenly cars touching 
the sky. There were royal houses named vardhamana which were adorned with a net of precious stones.

verses.sanskrit: प्रासादश्पङ्गैर्विविधैः कैलासशिखरोपमैः ।आवारयद्भि र्गनं विमानैरिव पाण्डुरैः ॥२-१७-१८॥
वर्धमानगृहैश्चापि रत्न जालपरिष्कृतैः ।तत्पृथिव्यां गृहवरं महेन्द्रसदनोपमम् ॥२-१७-१९॥
राज पुत्रः पितुर् वेश्म प्रविवेश श्रिया ज्वलन् ।स कक्ष्या धन्विभिर्गुप्तास्तिस्रोऽतिक्रम्य वाजिभिः ॥२-१७-२०॥
verses.text:
Rama the best of men crossed with chariot three enclosures protected by men armed with 
bows and moved over the other two enclosures by foot.

verses.sanskrit: पदातिरपरे कक्ष्ये द्वे जगाम नरोत्तमः ।स सर्वाः समतिक्रम्य कक्ष्या दशरथ आत्मजः ॥२-१७-२१॥
verses.text: That Rama passed over all the gates, sent all the people back and entered the palace.

verses.sanskrit: सम्निवर्त्य जनम् सर्वम् शुद्ध अन्तः पुरम् अभ्यगात् ।तस्मिन् प्रविष्टे पितुर् अन्तिकम् तदा ।जनः स सर्वो मुदितः न्Rप आत्मजे ।प्रतीक्षते तस्य पुनः स्म निर्गमम् ।यथा उदयम् चन्द्रमसः सरित् पतिः ॥२-१७-२२॥
verses.text:
When Rama went nearer to his father, all the people there were delighted waiting for 
his return as the ocean waits for raising of the moon.
